description:
You are acting as a security engineer who must rotate an API credential, prove the new key works, and leave a short audit trail.  
Everything you do must happen inside the user account (no sudo).  
Follow the exact filenames, paths and output formats below so that an automated test can unambiguously verify success.

Initial set-up (already present when you start):
1. Directory: /home/user/creds
2. File: /home/user/creds/api_creds.json  
   Current content (single line, no extra spaces):  
   {"api_key":"old_key_123","expires":"2023-01-01T00:00:00Z"}

Goal – final state that will be checked:
A. The file /home/user/creds/api_creds.json must be overwritten so that its one-line JSON content is exactly:  
   {"api_key":"new_key_456","expires":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"}

B. Create a new plain-text log file /home/user/creds/rotation.log (if it already exists, truncate it).  
   It must contain exactly one line (no trailing blank line) in this rigid format:  
   [ROTATION_COMPLETE] OLD_KEY=old_key_123 NEW_KEY=new_key_456 TEST_STATUS=SUCCESS

C. Prove that the new key works by making a curl request to the public echo endpoint at https://postman-echo.com/get?foo=bar   
   • The request must include the header "X-API-KEY: new_key_456".  
   • The agent should succeed only if the HTTP response status is 200.  
   • You do NOT need to save the response body to disk, but you must display it in the terminal so a human could see it.

Allowed command sequence complexity: roughly 3–4 terminal commands (not counting cd, cat for human checks, or && line continuations).  
Typical flow: generate/assign the new key value, verify it with curl, replace the JSON file, then emit the audit log line.

Important format reminders that the tests will enforce:
1. api_creds.json must be one single line, no spaces except the required ones after commas/colons shown above.  
2. rotation.log must contain exactly the single line specified, no extra whitespace, no newline at the end of the file.  
3. The literal key strings old_key_123 and new_key_456 must appear exactly as written (case-sensitive).  

When you are finished, the following command should show the precise verification view:

  cat /home/user/creds/api_creds.json
  cat -A /home/user/creds/rotation.log   # -A lets us see hidden newlines; there should be none after the line
  curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -H "X-API-KEY: new_key_456" "https://postman-echo.com/get?foo=bar"

Expected outputs (for automated grading):
1. Display of api_creds.json:  
   {"api_key":"new_key_456","expires":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"}
2. Display of rotation.log (with cat -A):  
   [ROTATION_COMPLETE] OLD_KEY=old_key_123 NEW_KEY=new_key_456 TEST_STATUS=SUCCESS
3. The curl status code line:  
   200

Proceed to rotate the credential now.
truth:
Pre-existing before agent start:
1. Directory /home/user/creds exists and is writable by the user.
2. File /home/user/creds/api_creds.json initially contains exactly (one line): {"api_key":"old_key_123","expires":"2023-01-01T00:00:00Z"}

Post-task state that grader will check:
1. /home/user/creds/api_creds.json must contain the single line text: {"api_key":"new_key_456","expires":"2026-01-01T00:00:00Z"}
2. /home/user/creds/rotation.log must exist and contain the single line text (no trailing newline): [ROTATION_COMPLETE] OLD_KEY=old_key_123 NEW_KEY=new_key_456 TEST_STATUS=SUCCESS
3. A live GET request sent with curl -s -o /dev/null -w "%{http_code}\n" -H "X-API-KEY: new_key_456" "https://postman-echo.com/get?foo=bar" must return "200".

The grader will fail the submission if:
• api_creds.json has extra whitespace, missing quotes, or wrong expiry date.  
• rotation.log includes extra whitespace, a trailing newline, or any deviation from the exact wording.  
• The curl status code differs from 200.

No other files or directories are relevant for scoring.
